title: Fee schedule - Canada.ca
content:
This section contains policy, procedures and guidance used by IRCC staff. It is posted on the department’s website as a courtesy to stakeholders.
- Adult, grant of citizenship, subsection 5(1) of the Act
- Minor, grant of citizenship, subsection 5(2) of the Act
- Adult, grant of citizenship, statelessness – bloodline connection, subsection 5(5) of the Act
- Adoptee, grant of citizenship, section 5.1 of the Act
- Adult only, renunciation of citizenship, section 9 of the Act
- Adult only, renunciation of citizenship, section R7.1 of the Regulations
- Resumption of citizenship, subsection 11(1) of the Act
- Adult and minor, proof of citizenship, section 3 of the Act
- Search of records, section 29 of the Regulations
- Processing fees Family class or spouse and common-law partners in Canada class Investor, entrepreneur or self-employed persons class Members of the permit holder's class Other classes
- Family class or spouse and common-law partners in Canada class
- Investor, entrepreneur or self-employed persons class
- Members of the permit holder's class
- Right of permanent residence fee (RPRF)
- Temporary resident visa
- Temporary resident permit
- Extension as a temporary resident (visitor)
- Restoration of temporary resident status
- Permanent resident card
- Certification and replacement of an immigration document
- Verification of status
- Authorization to return to Canada

## Citizenship (Effective January 1, 2015)
Right of Citizenship Footnote 1
Adult, grant of citizenship, subsection 5(1) of the Citizenship Act
Minor applying as an adult,  grant of citizenship, subsection A5(1)
n/a if minor at the time of application
Minor, grant of citizenship, subsection A5(2)
Adult, grant of citizenship, statelessness – bloodline connection, subsection A5(5)
n/a for minors $100 if adult at the time of application
Adoptee, grant of citizenship, section A5.1
$100 or $530 if adult at the time of application
n/a if minor at the time of application $100 if adult at the time of application
$100 or $630 if adult at the time of application
Adult only, renunciation of citizenship, section A9
Adult only, renunciation of citizenship , section 7.1 of the Citizenship Regulations
Resumption of citizenship, subsection A11(1)
Adult and minor, proof of citizenship, section A3
Search of records, section R29 of the Citizenship Regulations

### Permanent resident [R295] – Family Class or Spouse and Common-Law Partner in Canada Class
The sponsorship fee [R304(1)] is always $85 is paid per application is lower for the principal applicant only because of the sponsorship fee is not refunded if the sponsor is found ineligible to sponsor
The sponsorship fee [R304(1)]
- is paid per application
- is lower for the principal applicant only because of the sponsorship fee
- is not refunded if the sponsor is found ineligible to sponsor
The principal applicant is a spouse, common-law partner or conjugal partner mother, father, grandmother or grandfather dependent child of the sponsor who is over 22 years of age or who is married or in a common-law or conjugal relationship, regardless of age
The principal applicant is a
- spouse, common-law partner or conjugal partner
- mother, father, grandmother or grandfather
- dependent child of the sponsor who is over 22 years of age or who is married or in a common-law or conjugal relationship, regardless of age
The principal applicant is under 22 a dependent child of the sponsor (orphan or child to be adopted) not a spouse, common-law partner or conjugal partner
The principal applicant is
- a dependent child of the sponsor (orphan or child to be adopted)
- not a spouse, common-law partner or conjugal partner
The family member of the principal applicant is either 22 years of age or older, or under 22 and is a spouse or common-law partner
The family member of the principal applicant is either
- 22 years of age or older, or
- under 22 and is a spouse or common-law partner
The family member of the principal applicant is under 22 years of age, and is not a spouse or common-law partner
The family member of the principal applicant is
- under 22 years of age, and
- is not a spouse or common-law partner

### Quebec investors and entrepreneurs, start-up visa or self-employed persons class
The family member of the principal applicant is either 22 years or older; or less than 22 years and is a spouse or common-law partner.
The family member of the principal applicant is either
- 22 years or older; or
- less than 22 years and is a spouse or common-law partner.
The family member of the principal applicant is under 22 years of age, and not a spouse or common-law partner.
The family member of the principal applicant is
- under 22 years of age, and
- not a spouse or common-law partner.
Note: If a dependent child has a spouse or a common-law partner, that spouse cannot be included on this application as only family members can be processed. The spouse of the dependent child will have to be sponsored later through the family class.

### Members of the permit holders class
Application by a person as a member of the permit holders class to remain in Canada as a permanent resident
            [R302]
Application by a person as a member of the permit holders class to remain in Canada as a permanent resident
            [R302]

- Protected persons in Canada (Convention refugees and positive pre-removal risk assessments)
- Temporary public policy for out-of-status construction workers in the Greater Toronto Area
- Permanent residence pathways for Hong Kong residents
Principal applicant (R307 for H&C)
Principal applicant (R307 for H&C)
The family member of the principal applicant is either either 22 years of age or older, or less than 22 years and is a spouse or common-law partner.
The family member of the principal applicant is either either
- 22 years of age or older, or
- less than 22 years and is a spouse or common-law partner.
The family member of the principal applicant is under 22 years of age, and not a spouse or common-law partner
The family member of the principal applicant is
- under 22 years of age, and
- not a spouse or common-law partner

Principal applicant (R307)
Principal applicant (R307)
The family member of the principal applicant is 22 years of age or older, or less than 22 years and is a spouse or common-law partner.
The family member of the principal applicant is
- 22 years of age or older, or
- less than 22 years and is a spouse or common-law partner.
The family member of the principal applicant is under 22 years of age, and not a spouse or common-law partner

- Protected persons outside of Canada: Convention refugee abroad class (including source country and country of asylum) are exempt from processing fees [R295(2) (a) ].
- Protected temporary residents in Canada and the family members included in their application are exempt from processing fees [R301 (1.1)].
- Economic Mobility Pathways Pilot: Applicants and the family members are exempt from the processing fees (Public policy to support the Economic Mobility Pathways Pilot [EMPP] – Phase 2).

### Right of permanent residence Fee (RPRF)
All applicants in any immigration class must pay the RPRF, except for the following: protected persons (including Convention refugees) who apply for permanent residence in any class and the family members included in their application members of the protected temporary resident class and the family members included in their application dependent children regardless of age, of a sponsor or principal applicant dependent children of dependent children of a sponsor or principal applicant a child to be adopted, an orphaned brother, sister, niece, nephew or grandchild Note: A dependent child, as defined in R2, includes the adopted child of the parent. Therefore, the child would be exempt from the RPRF [R303] H&C principal applicants, regardless of age, must pay RPRF except for protected persons and their family members a principal applicant who is a “dependent child” of a Canadian citizen or permanent resident
All applicants in any immigration class must pay the RPRF, except for the following:
- protected persons (including Convention refugees) who apply for permanent residence in any class and the family members included in their application
- members of the protected temporary resident class and the family members included in their application
- dependent children regardless of age, of a sponsor or principal applicant
- dependent children of dependent children of a sponsor or principal applicant
- a child to be adopted, an orphaned brother, sister, niece, nephew or grandchild
Note: A dependent child, as defined in R2, includes the adopted child of the parent. Therefore, the child would be exempt from the RPRF [R303] H&C principal applicants, regardless of age, must pay RPRF except for
- protected persons and their family members
- a principal applicant who is a “dependent child” of a Canadian citizen or permanent resident

### Processing fees for temporary residence
Temporary resident visa – Single or multiple entry R296(1); R297(1); R296(2); lists the exceptions Note: Includes Parent and Grandparent Extended Stay Temporary Resident Visa super visa ) applications received at visa office (no fee required for TRV exempt nationals applying at a visa office).
Temporary resident visa – Maximum family rate [R297(2)]
Work permit R299(1); R299(2) lists exceptions In-Canada Refugee Claimant – Fees Chart
Work permit Maximum fee for a group of three or more performing artists and their staff [R299(3)]
Open work permit holder
Employer compliance maximum fee
Study permit R300(1); R300(2) lists the exceptions In-Canada Refugee Claimant – Fees Chart
Temporary resident permit R298(1); R298(2) lists the exceptions
Extension of authorization to remain in Canada as a temporary resident (visitor) R305(1); R305(2) lists the exceptions
Restoration of temporary resident status [R306(1)] Note: Restoration does not apply to expired TRP holders.
Note: Persons, other than a group of performing artists and their staff, who apply at the same time and place for a work permit or a study permit do not pay the fee for a temporary resident visa [R296(2)(d)].
To be eligible for the employer compliance maximum fee, the employer must make offers of employment to a group of three or more foreign nationals, consisting of performing artists and their staff, at the same time.
Permanent resident card (in-Canada only) R308
Permanent resident card (in-Canada only) R308
Travel document (overseas only) [R315] Client must obtain this at a mission. It is issued to a permanent resident who needs to travel to Canada and is not in possession of a permanent resident card. Reference: A31(3)
Travel document (overseas only) [R315] Client must obtain this at a mission. It is issued to a permanent resident who needs to travel to Canada and is not in possession of a permanent resident card. Reference: A31(3)
Certification and replacement of an immigration document R311 Note: The fee to replace a permanent residence card is $50.
Certification and replacement of an immigration document R311 Note: The fee to replace a permanent residence card is $50.
Verification of status R311 (3)
Verification of status R311 (3)
Authorization to return to Canada R310
Authorization to return to Canada R310
Rehabilitation - Serious Criminality R309(a)
Rehabilitation - Serious Criminality R309(a)
Rehabilitation - Criminality R309(b)
Rehabilitation - Criminality R309(b)
Removal costs: Repayment for removal to the United States or Saint-Pierre and Miquelon
Removal costs: Repayment for removal to the United States or Saint-Pierre and Miquelon
Removal costs: Repayment for removal to any other destination
Removal costs: Repayment for removal to any other destination
Note: The handling of public monies  does not apply to visa offices, local Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) or citizenship applications from abroad.
